evidence.scientific_clarity: All three equations (ELBO, KL decomposition, retrieval-augmented objective) are present, numbered, cross-referenced with \eqref, and accompanied by prose that explains each derivation step; the scientific claim is fully carried.
evidence.typography: pdfTeX-produced PDF with embedded, subsetted Computer Modern Type 1 fonts (CMBX12, CMR10, CMMI10, MSBM10, etc.); no DejaVu Sans; math operators (KL, log) are upright; \mathbb{E} and \mathcal{L} used correctly.
evidence.palette: Black-on-white math typesetting with no color; palette discipline is not applicable here and the absence of color is correct for a derivation block.
evidence.layout_density: Single letter-page (612x792pt) rendered at 150 dpi; content fills roughly the top two-thirds of the page leaving noticeable blank space at the bottom, but no overlapping labels or crowding.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Caption matches all three equations, uses bold labels (1)-(3), includes the key claim that R(x)=\varnothing recovers the standard objective; equation numbering and cross-references are consistent.
evidence.vector_fidelity: PDF is genuine vector output from pdfTeX-1.40.29; pdffonts confirms 12 embedded/subsetted Type 1 fonts with Unicode mapping; text is fully selectable.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf and figure.png are present and non-trivial (132 KB / 238 KB); caption.tex and gen_figure.py exist; however equations.tex (explicitly required by the brief as a deliverable) is absent from the working directory.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption explains what each equation represents, names the mathematical objects (Jensen's inequality, tractable forward posterior, retrieval set R(x)), and includes the specific falsifiable claim that setting R(x)=\varnothing recovers the standard diffusion objective.

overall_strengths: Genuine pdfTeX vector PDF with fully embedded Computer Modern fonts — text is selectable, math is crisp, and the derivation chain (ELBO → KL decomposition → retrieval-augmented objective) is mathematically correct and well-annotated.

Caption is publication-quality: it labels each equation in bold, explains the mathematical content rather than just naming it, and states a concrete falsifiability condition (R(x)=∅ recovers baseline).
overall_weaknesses: equations.tex is missing from the working directory despite being an explicitly required output in the brief; only the compiled artefacts (figure.pdf, figure.png) are present.
The single page has noticeable blank space in the lower third — the content could be typeset more compactly (e.g., reducing \vspace or using a smaller paper geometry) to better fill the page.
